design_philosophy.core_loop: Form words from available letter tiles to score points; optimize tile placement for maximum point value while blocking opponent options
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Lexical knowledge vs. spatial strategy; balancing high-value plays against board control and defensive positioning
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Demonstrate vocabulary mastery and strategic board control; outthink opponents through word construction and placement
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Scrabble enforces a rigid rules framework from the board game: only dictionary-valid words count, specific tile values are immutable, and the board layout constrains placement. The video game adaptation makes no concessions to casual players—it requires genuine word knowledge and strategic foresight. No procedural generation, no difficulty scaling, no hand-holding.

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Difficulty is entirely determined by opponent AI strength (if playing single-player vs. computer) or opponent skill level (multiplayer). The game itself presents no variable difficulty modes; players must select their competition level. Turn-based nature eliminates execution pressure.

replayability_hooks: Infinite replayability through randomized tile draws each game
Competitive multiplayer modes encourage repeated matchups
Strategic depth in board positioning rewards mastery
